content:
# Final Review Arbiter
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Rejected the disputed amendment. The code and tests show that all-completed same-bead history is intentionally recovered by adopting a deterministic completed project and running `SyncMilestone` to reconcile stale controller state; changing that branch to return `None` would break duplicate-conflict recovery when the default completed project already exists.

## Rulings

- `fr-13-aa34a9ab`: Reject
  The cited behavior is real, but the defect claim does not hold. `find_existing_bead_project` intentionally returns a completed candidate when every same-bead match is completed (`src/cli/project.rs:923-930`), `ensure_project_for_controller` adopts it (`src/cli/milestone.rs:1146-1157`), and `load_run_action` maps that state to `SyncMilestone` (`src/cli/milestone.rs:1218-1226`). That sync path is not suppressing work arbitrarily: it reconciles the bead outcome back into milestone state and advances the controller to `Selecting` or `Completed` (`src/cli/run.rs:947-965`, `1500-1542`). The implementation is also covered by explicit tests for deterministic completed-project recovery (`src/cli/project.rs:3569-3595`) and controller recovery from all-completed same-bead history (`src/cli/milestone.rs:3020-3088`). The tipping evidence against the amendment is the proposed `return None` remediation: `ensure_project_for_controller` would fall through to `execute_create_from_bead`, which still uses the default bead project id; completed same-bead history is allowed by `reject_existing_same_bead_project_creation` (`src/cli/project.rs:623-645`), but the existing default-id project then triggers `DuplicateProject` (`src/cli/project.rs:435-456`). Recovery from that duplicate depends on `find_existing_bead_project` returning `Some` (`src/cli/milestone.rs:1175-1205`), so changing the all-completed branch to `None` would convert the common stale-controller case into `controller could not recover the existing project for bead ...`. This is a deliberate recovery strategy, not a correctness regression.
